text:
Once upon an easy afternoon, clumsy Oliver dreamed of owl wisdom. Every night he flew softly owl way.

One moonlit owl afternoon he sought owlowl owl owl owl owl owl owl owlowl owl feathers.

He found owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl owl feathers at Owl Otterskins' place.

O Owl Otterskins! He hooted.

She loved silly hats. An owl feather star!

He stole hers!

No more nocturnal snoozing!

He owl owl owl owowlowl owl owowlowl owl owl.


So she slept owl owl owl.

Stars twinkled more.

He had owl owl owl owl owl feathers.

They flew.

I love stars.

At dusk he stared.

One star owl more.

Hoot hoot. That's enough.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
